question.text: The SEBI IA Regulations mandate client-level segregation of advisory and distribution activities. Which statement is correct?

answer.text: The same client can receive both advisory and distribution services from the same group entity
A client can either be an advisory client or a distribution client — not both at the group level
Advisory and distribution services can be provided to the same client if separate fees are charged
Client-level segregation applies only to individual investment advisers

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI mandates strict client-level segregation at the group level — a client can either be an advisory client (where no distributor consideration is received) or a distribution services client (where no advisory fee is collected). The same client cannot receive both. **The Trap:** Separate fees don't help — the segregation is at the client level, not the service level. This applies to both individual and non-individual IAs. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is a key differentiator tested in Series X-A and X-B. Series V-A covers the distribution side of this segregation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When onboarding a new client, make a clear election upfront — advisory or distribution. Switching a client from one to the other requires proper process and documentation.
